name: Potatoes Nicoise
recipeIngredient: 3 medium Potatoes, sliced thin (about 1/4 inch thick)
3 Red onions (about 1 lb), peeled and thinly sliced
1/4 cup (or more) Shredded low fat Swiss or Cheddar cheese
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F. Cut garlic in half lengthwise. Rub the inside of a 1½ quart shallow baking dish with the cut garlic.
2. Peel potatoes and cut into ¼ inch slices. Place in a bowl of cold water.
3. Peel tomatoes, then cut into ½ thick slices. Place in a bowl of cold water to chill quickly (place in boiling water for 30 seconds, then immerse immediately in ice water).
4. In a small bowl, combine parsley, salt, tarragon, basil, and nutmeg.
5. Drain potatoes well and pat dry with paper towels. Sprinkle half the potato slices into the prepared casserole dish (spray first with non-stick spray). Sprinkle with half the parsley mixture. Add half the onions and half the tomatoes.
6. Sprinkle tomatoes with ½ teaspoon salt. Repeat layering procedure. Cut margarine into small pieces and dot onto the casserole. Cover loosely with foil.
7. Bake for 45 minutes or until potatoes are fork-tender. Sprinkle cheese on top, then bake uncovered for 5 minutes or until cheese is melted and lightly browned.
